steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The LNH predicts a negative correlation between exotericity and grammatical complexity (fusion and informativity). To be inconsistent with the LNH, the data must show the opposite—a positive or non-negative correlation between exotericity and those complexity indices.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: describes a finding that does not compare exotericity with fusion or informativity—does not address the LNH prediction. ✗
  • Choice B: describes a negative correlation consistent with what the LNH predicts. ✗
  • Choice C: reports a slightly positive correlation between fusion and exotericity and between informativity and exotericity—directly contradicting the LNH's predicted inverse relationship. ✓
  • Choice D: describes correlations among complexity indices themselves, not between exotericity and complexity. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
